name: David Bowie
abstract: David Robert Jones (8 January 1947 – 10 January 2016), known as David Bowie, was an English singer, songwriter and actor. Regarded as among the most influential musicians of the 20th century, he was known for his constant reinvention and visual presentation, and is often referred to as the "chameleon of rock".

about_quotes.speaker: Robert Glasper
about_quotes.evidence: And the David Bowie album “Space © Fillius Jazz Archive -18- Oddity,” it’s a very obscure album. But I recorded the song on there called “Letter to Hermione” for “Black Radio” 1 back in 2011.
about_quotes.doc_title: Robert Glasper interviewed by Greg Thomas, Clinton, New York, February 20, 2016

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions David Bowie in the context of Carlos Alomar's work history ('He only talks about having worked with David Bowie'). Kenny Barron does not mention David Bowie, nor does he claim any personal or professional relationship with him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: Pasadena Roof Orchestra were hired to do the music for a film called “Just a Gigolo” which starred the totally unlikely combination of David Bowie and Marlene Dietrich.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: Mike Garson got me the audition. He was David Bowie’s conductor for, I don’t know, forever.
